title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: But {{c1::the economic impact seems}}(经济影响似乎) to {{c2::be dramatically behind}}(远远落后). It’s very difficult to {{c3::make sense of}}(理解), how can the model, on the one hand, do these amazing things, and then on the other hand, repeat itself twice in some situation?
original: But the economic impact seems to be dramatically behind. It’s very difficult to make sense of, how can the model, on the one hand, do these amazing things, and then on the other hand, repeat itself twice in some situation?
translation: 但经济影响似乎远远落后。很难理解，模型怎么可能在一方面做出这些令人惊叹的事情，另一方面却在某些情况下重复自己两次？
note:
phrases: the economic impact seems | be dramatically behind | make sense of
title: Ilya-Sutskever
source: 
chunk: chunk_0005_f794b9
source_lang: en
target_lang: zh
phrase_types: discourse_organizer
transfer: Useful for organizing argument flow across longer explanations.
expression_transfer: Useful for organizing argument flow across longer explanations.

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: There’ll be very strong {{c1::economic forces for}}(经济力量) this, and I think the impact is going to be felt very strongly. Dwarkesh Patel When do you {{c2::expect that impact}}(预期会产生影响)?
original: There’ll be very strong economic forces for this, and I think the impact is going to be felt very strongly. Dwarkesh Patel When do you expect that impact?
translation: 这背后会有非常强大的经济力量推动，我认为其影响将会被深刻感受到。Dwarkesh Patel 你预计这种影响会在什么时候出现？
note:
phrases: economic forces for | expect that impact
title: Ilya-Sutskever
source: 
chunk: chunk_0003_b07743
source_lang: en
target_lang: zh
phrase_types: stance_positioning
transfer: Useful for signaling your stance before giving evidence.
expression_transfer: Useful for signaling your stance before giving evidence.

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: I have {{c1::two possible explanations}}(两种可能的解释). {{c2::The more whimsical explanation}}(更离奇的解释) is that maybe {{c3::RL training makes}}(强化学习训练会) the models {{c4::a little too single-minded}}(有点过于专注) and narrowly focused, {{c5::a little bit too unaware}}(有点过于不自知), even though it also makes them aware in some other ways.
original: I have two possible explanations. The more whimsical explanation is that maybe RL training makes the models a little too single-minded and narrowly focused, a little bit too unaware, even though it also makes them aware in some other ways.
translation: 我有两个可能的解释。更有趣的解释是，也许强化学习训练让模型变得过于专注和狭隘，在某种程度上让它们变得不那么敏感，尽管它也让它们在其他方面变得更加敏感。
note:
phrases: two possible explanations | The more whimsical explanation | RL training makes | a little too single-minded | a little bit too unaware
title: Ilya-Sutskever
source:

target_lang: zh

transfer: Useful for acknowledging a point before pivoting to contrast.
expression_transfer: Useful for acknowledging a point before pivoting to contrast.

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: Dwarkesh Patel {{c1::Another thing that’s}}(还有另一个问题是) crazy is how normal the slow takeoff feels. The idea that we’d be investing 1% of GDP in AI, {{c2::I feel like}}(我觉得) it would have felt like {{c3::a bigger deal}}(更大的影响), whereas right now it just feels...
original: Dwarkesh Patel Another thing that’s crazy is how normal the slow takeoff feels. The idea that we’d be investing 1% of GDP in AI, I feel like it would have felt like a bigger deal, whereas right now it just feels...
translation: Dwarkesh Patel 另一个令人难以置信的是，缓慢的起步感是如此的正常。我们投入1%的GDP用于AI这一想法，我感觉这本应该是一件大事，然而现在却只是觉得...
note:
phrases: Another thing that’s | I feel like | a bigger deal
title: Ilya-Sutskever

target_lang: zh

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: Ilya Sutskever — {{c1::We're moving from}}(我们正在从) the age of scaling to the age of research – Explaining model jaggedness Ilya Sutskever {{c2::You know what’s}}(你知道什么最) crazy? Dwarkesh Patel Meaning what?
original: Ilya Sutskever — We're moving from the age of scaling to the age of research – Explaining model jaggedness Ilya Sutskever You know what’s crazy? Dwarkesh Patel Meaning what?
translation: 伊利亚·苏茨克弗——我们正从规模化时代进入研究时代——解释模型锯齿状现象 伊利亚·苏茨克弗 你知道什么最疯狂吗？ 德瓦雷什·帕特尔 什么意思？
note:
phrases: We're moving from | You know what’s

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: From what I hear, all the companies have teams that just produce {{c1::new RL environments}}(新的强化学习环境) and just add it to {{c2::the training mix}}(训练混合). The question is, well, what are those?
original: From what I hear, all the companies have teams that just produce new RL environments and just add it to the training mix. The question is, well, what are those?
translation: 据我所闻，所有公司都有团队专门开发新的强化学习环境，并将其加入训练混合中。问题是，那些环境究竟是什么？
note:
phrases: new RL environments | the training mix
title: Ilya-Sutskever

source_lang: en
target_lang: zh
phrase_types: reusable_high_frequency_chunk
transfer: Useful as a reusable chunk for fluent topic transitions.
expression_transfer: Useful as a reusable chunk for fluent topic transitions.

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: Then you {{c1::tell the model}}(告诉模型), “Can you please {{c2::fix the bug}}(修复这个错误)?” And the model says, “Oh my God, you’re so right. I have a bug. Let me {{c3::go fix that}}(去修复那个).”
original: Then you tell the model, “Can you please fix the bug?” And the model says, “Oh my God, you’re so right. I have a bug. Let me go fix that.”
translation: 然后你告诉模型：“能不能帮忙修复这个bug？”模型就会说：“哦我的天，您说得对。我确实有个bug。让我去修复它。”
note:
phrases: tell the model | fix the bug | go fix that
title: Ilya-Sutskever

source_lang: en
target_lang: zh

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: Yeah. This is {{c1::one of the very confusing things}}(其中最令人困惑的事情之一) about {{c2::the models right now}}(目前的模型). How to reconcile the fact that they are doing so well on evals?
original: Yeah. This is one of the very confusing things about the models right now. How to reconcile the fact that they are doing so well on evals?
translation: 是的。这正是当前模型中非常令人困惑的一点：如何解释它们在评估中表现如此出色的事实？
note:
phrases: one of the very confusing things | the models right now

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: Then you tell it, “You {{c1::have this new}}(拥有这个全新的) second bug,” and it tells you, “Oh my God, how {{c2::could I have done}}(我本可以做到) it? You’re {{c3::so right again}}(又对了),”
original: Then you tell it, “You have this new second bug,” and it tells you, “Oh my God, how could I have done it? You’re so right again,”
translation: 然后你告诉它：“你又出现了一个新的第二个错误。”它就会说：“天哪，我怎么会犯这种错呢？你又是对的。”
note:
phrases: have this new | could I have done | so right again

title: Ilya-Sutskever

translation: 这怎么可能呢？我不确定，但这确实表明有些奇怪的事情正在发生。

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: Ilya Sutskever Sure. Dwarkesh Patel I think your point, about how from the average person’s point of view {{c1::nothing is that different}}(并没有什么不同), will {{c2::continue being true}}(继续保持真实) {{c3::even into the singularity}}(即使在奇点之后).
original: Ilya Sutskever Sure. Dwarkesh Patel I think your point, about how from the average person’s point of view nothing is that different, will continue being true even into the singularity.
translation: 伊利亚·苏茨克弗：当然。德瓦尔凯什·帕特尔：我认为你的观点，即从普通人的角度来看，没有什么不同，这个观点即使在奇点到来时也会继续成立。
note:
phrases: nothing is that different | continue being true | even into the singularity

title: Ilya-Sutskever

translation: 我认为AI的影响将会显现出来。AI将会在经济中得到广泛应用。

translation: 我认为这些模型看起来比它们的经济影响所暗示的更聪明。——伊利亚·苏茨克弗

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: Ilya Sutskever The thing which I was referring to not feeling different is, okay, such and such company announced some difficult-to-comprehend {{c1::dollar amount of investment}}(投资金额). I don’t think anyone knows what to do with that.
original: Ilya Sutskever The thing which I was referring to not feeling different is, okay, such and such company announced some difficult-to-comprehend dollar amount of investment. I don’t think anyone knows what to do with that.
translation: 伊利亚·苏茨克弗 我提到的那种没有感到不同的情况是，比如某某公司宣布了一笔难以理解的巨额投资。我认为没有人知道该如何利用这些资金。
note:
phrases: dollar amount of investment

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: Back when people were doing pre-training, {{c1::the question of what}}(关于什么的问题) data to train on was answered, because that answer was everything. When you do pre-training, {{c2::you need all}}(你需要所有) the data.
original: Back when people were doing pre-training, the question of what data to train on was answered, because that answer was everything. When you do pre-training, you need all the data.
translation: 在预训练阶段，人们并不需要考虑训练数据的选择问题，因为答案很简单：所有数据。预训练需要尽可能多的数据。
note:
phrases: the question of what | you need all
title: Ilya-Sutskever

target_lang: zh

expression_transfer: Useful as a reusable chunk for fluent topic transitions.

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: But when people do {{c1::RL training}}(强化学习训练), they do need to think. They say, “Okay, we want to have {{c2::this kind of RL training}}(这种强化学习训练) for this thing and that kind of RL training for that thing.”
original: But when people do RL training, they do need to think. They say, “Okay, we want to have this kind of RL training for this thing and that kind of RL training for that thing.”
translation: 然而，在强化学习（RL）训练阶段，人们需要认真思考。他们会说：“好的，我们想要这种类型的RL训练用于这个任务，而那种类型的RL训练用于那个任务。”
note:
phrases: RL training | this kind of RL training
title: Ilya-Sutskever

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: An example would be, {{c1::let’s say}}(假设) you {{c2::use vibe coding}}(使用VIBE编码) to do something. You {{c3::go to some place}}(去某个地方) and then you {{c4::get a bug}}(遇到一个错误).
original: An example would be, let’s say you use vibe coding to do something. You go to some place and then you get a bug.
translation: 例如，假设你使用了vibe编码来做某件事。你来到某个地方，然后遇到了一个bug。

original: Ilya Sutskever We get used to things pretty fast, it turns out. But also it’s kind of abstract.
translation: 伊利亚·苏茨克弗 我们发现自己能够非常快地适应新事物。不过，这也有些抽象。

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: You look at the evals and you go, “Those are {{c1::pretty hard evals}}(相当难的评估).” They are {{c2::doing so well}}(表现这么好). But the economic impact seems to be dramatically behind.
